div1.entryFree:
Ànnoy, subst., pain, suffering, grief: life was death's a. Ven. 497. worse than Tantalus' is her a. Ven. 497 mirth doth search the bottom of a. Lucr. 1109. threatening Ilion with a. Lucr. 1109 receivest with pleasure thine a., Sonn. 8, 4. farewell sour a. H6C V, 7, 45. rape was root of thine a. Tit. IV, 1, 49.
2) injury, harm: good angels guard thee from the boar's a. R3 V, 3, 156.
